complete_examples.overview: Example workflows showing how the Research mode handles typical investigation tasks.

example.name: trace_feature_flow
example.scenario: User wants to understand how the attendance (điểm danh) feature works end-to-end.
example.user_request: How does the daily attendance feature work? Trace the full flow.

step.approach: Use semantic search for "điểm danh hàng ngày" or "daily attendance" to find the Vue component. Check hrm_FE/src/components/CHAMCONG/ directory listing.

step.description: Read the component and identify API calls
step.approach: Read the component file, focusing on imports from src/apis/ and function calls. Identify which API module and functions are used.
step.expected_outcome: List of API functions called and their parameters

step.description: Trace through the API module to the backend
step.approach: Read the API module to find endpoint URLs. Search for matching FastAPI route definitions in hrm_BE/.
step.expected_outcome: Mapped frontend API calls to backend endpoints

step.description: Read the backend route and query registry
step.approach: Read the router handler. Identify query keys used. Read hrm_BE/app/queries/diemdanhhangngay_queries.py for the actual SQL.
step.expected_outcome: Full understanding of the SQL operations and data transformations

step.approach: Present the complete flow as a numbered trace with file references at each stage.

example.completion: Present a structured trace showing: Component → API function → HTTP endpoint → Router handler → Query key → SQL query → Response shape → Frontend processing. Each step includes clickable file references.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Always trace through the query registry to see the actual SQL
Note auth dependencies and permission requirements at each layer

example.name: find_pattern_usage
example.scenario: User wants to understand how batch operations are implemented across the codebase.
example.user_request: What pattern do batch operations follow? Show me examples from different modules.

step.approach: Use semantic search for "batch operation" and "bulk insert". Use regex search for "batch" or "rows_affected" across backend files.
step.expected_outcome: List of files implementing batch operations

step.approach: Select batch handlers from different modules (e.g., attendance, salary, HR). Read each to understand the common structure.
step.expected_outcome: Understanding of the shared pattern and any variations

step.approach: Build a markdown table comparing: endpoint, payload structure, transaction behavior, response format, error handling across the selected modules.

example.completion: Present a comparison table of batch implementations with a summary of the common pattern and any notable deviations. Include file references for each example.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Compare multiple implementations to identify the dominant pattern
Note deviations — they may be intentional or candidates for standardization

example.name: answer_architecture_question
example.scenario: User wants to understand the authentication and session management architecture.
example.user_request: How does auth work? JWT, heartbeat, session cleanup — explain the full picture.

step.approach: Read the known auth-related files: security.py, session_cleanup.py, authStores.js, axiosInterceptor.js, Login.vue, apiLogin.js.
step.expected_outcome: Understanding of each component's role in the auth system

step.approach: Follow the login from UI form submission through API call, backend validation, JWT creation, token storage, and session initialization.
Find the heartbeat timer in the frontend, the heartbeat endpoint in the backend, and the cleanup mechanism for stale sessions.
Present a structured overview with sections for: Login, Token Management, Heartbeat, Session Cleanup, Permission Loading, and Error Handling (401/403).

example.completion: Present a multi-section architecture overview with flow descriptions and file references. Include timing details (heartbeat interval, cleanup threshold) found in the code.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Auth spans both frontend and backend — trace both sides
Include timing constants and configuration values in the findings
